novel_features.name: Code usage verifier against documented symbols
novel_features.description: Check Python code imports and qualified names against documented Claude Agent SDK symbols.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining local Python identifiers with a docs-derived symbol index.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before trusting generated SDK code or reviewing a PR that imports claude_agent_sdk.

novel_features.name: Agent-ready task context bundle
novel_features.description: Build a compact source-cited docs bundle for one SDK implementation task.
novel_features.rationale: Requires searching fetched docs sections, symbols, examples, and guide links together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs exact docs context without loading full Markdown pages.

novel_features.name: Doc change diff by content hash
novel_features.description: Compare current docs entity hashes against an optional baseline file.
novel_features.rationale: Requires entity-level hashing of fetched pages, sections, symbols, examples, and links.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to detect whether SDK docs facts have changed against a saved baseline.

novel_features.name: Minimal recipe composer from examples and signatures
novel_features.description: Compose a deterministic implementation scaffold from documented snippets and exact signatures.
novel_features.rationale: Requires combining extracted examples with the symbol index without inventing API calls.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need a copyable starting point constrained to documented SDK patterns.

novel_features.name: SDK surface map by entity type
novel_features.description: Map functions, classes, types, options, tools, hooks, and message blocks by entity type.
novel_features.rationale: Requires parsing the docs hierarchy and symbols into a local typed surface inventory.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to discover the available SDK surface before choosing an implementation path.

novel_features.name: Example-to-symbol coverage map
novel_features.description: Report which documented symbols have extracted examples and which do not.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining extracted code blocks against the local symbol table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to find example-backed SDK APIs and documentation coverage gaps.

novel_features.name: Anchor and link integrity audit
novel_features.description: Validate internal anchors, guide links, and section references in the fetched docs corpus.
novel_features.rationale: Requires page, heading, anchor, and link extraction across the docs corpus.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before relying on context bundles or citations in generated code reviews.

narrative.headline: A source-grounded CLI for exploring and verifying the Claude Agent SDK Python docs.
narrative.value_prop: Fetch the raw Claude Code docs index, search the Python Agent SDK reference, and extract exact symbols, examples, and citations. Novel verification and context commands help agents use the SDK without guessing.

quickstart.comment: Verify the CLI and local configuration without network or docs writes.
Fetch the docs index and Python Agent SDK page through the generated docs endpoints.
Find the exact docs page and section for an SDK option or type.
Read a symbol with its anchor, examples, and citations.
Build a compact task bundle for an agent implementing SDK custom tools.

troubleshoots.fix: Try a more specific SDK symbol, such as `ClaudeSDKClient` or `ClaudeAgentOptions`.
Run `claude-agent-sdk-python-docs-pp-cli map --kind classes,types,options --agent` to inspect the parsed docs surface.

troubleshoots.symptom: Generated code uses an undocumented SDK name.
troubleshoots.fix: Run `claude-agent-sdk-python-docs-pp-cli verify ./path --agent` and follow the cited replacement or docs link.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need exact Claude Agent SDK Python documentation, examples, signatures, or citations in an agent-friendly shape. It is strongest for implementation planning, code review, and preventing SDK hallucinations.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to run Claude Agent SDK applications.
Do not use this CLI for TypeScript SDK questions unless the docs mirror has been expanded to TypeScript pages.
Do not use this CLI as a replacement for live Claude Code authentication or account setup.

recipes.title: Find the right option fields for a client setup
recipes.explanation: Returns only the high-signal fields an agent needs instead of the full reference page.

recipes.title: Build a custom-tools implementation bundle
recipes.explanation: Pairs `--agent` with `--select` so downstream agents get just examples, section titles, and citations.

recipes.title: Verify a Python project against current docs
recipes.explanation: Flags undocumented Claude Agent SDK Python identifiers with source citations.

recipes.title: Inspect documented SDK surface area
recipes.explanation: Shows the SDK inventory by entity type before writing code.

trigger_phrases: look up Claude Agent SDK Python
verify this against Claude Agent SDK docs
use claude agent sdk python docs
run claude agent sdk python docs
